involvements.title: LifeTagger Inc.
involvements.body: LifeTagger is a proximity based tool that allows users to connect with the location surrounding them, strengthening customer engagement of several local businesses. My experience with the company over 4 years includes working with libraries such as React.js, Node.js, Android App Development, Flutter, and Parse Cloud in order to build solutions on the web, in mobile apps, and a backend server connecting it all together. Using these skills, I was able to not only increase customer engagement with registered companies, but also aid the victims of COVID-19 and Hurricane Ida with real-time statistics and relief resources.

involvements.title: Wisconsin Autonomous
involvements.body: As a part of the AutoDrive Challenge Series II developing a level 4 autonomous vehicle, Wisconsin Autonomous is focussed on converting a standard Chevy Bolt EUV into a fully autonomous vehicle capable of avoiding obstacles. At my short time so far, I implemented ROS2 into autonomous vehicle structure to integrate components through the vehicle, ensuring that path planning and rerouting can be done at will. Additionally, I developed high-level and low-level path planning and path following algorithms and implemented them into a Chevrolet Bolt EUV.

involvements.title: RoboLoCo
involvements.body: Although I wasn't able to join the team until my last year of high-school, I became a part of the leadership team as the co-lead in a software role. What began by just recruiting and mentoring 80+ new students in the off-season, grew into competing at a high level in the chesapeake district of the FIRST Robotics Competition (FRC). Throughout the phase of the competitions, RoboLoCo was able to win the Engineering Inspiration award at the 2022 district championships qualifying us for the world championship in Houston, TX.

involvements.title: Wando Advanced Robotics (Phoenix)
involvements.body: Coming in as a freshman in high school, I joined this FIRST Robotics Competition (FRC) team looking to learn. Quickly, as I learned more, I supervised the development of vision software, mobile scouting apps, and electrical boards for competitions and ded the development of competition-ready robot code.
